metadata.title:
MORE LIGHT IS SHED


ON BRIDGE SITUA TON

pages.raw_text:
After a lengthy discussion by
the city council last night as to the
manner in which City Engineer
Ralston is building the piers and
foundation for the new Monroe
street bridge, the council seemed
ready to adopt a resolution to can
Gel its previous order instructing
the engineer to proceed by day
labor and, instead, have the whole
bridge built in one general con-
tract.


The fact developed in the discus
sion that the. city engineer wanted
the city to authorize the purchase
of cable equipment worth $4,000
to $4,000 to lay 1000 cubic Yards
OF concrete that reputable private
contractors have stated they are
willing 10 put in for from 81 to So
per cubic yard. or $4,000 to s32, 900
for the work.


City Engineer Ralston was not
present in person at the meeting
and sent as substitutes three of
the young engineers in charge of
the work, who have previously
been referred to on the floor of
the council as members of Mr..
Ralston's kindergarten engineer
ing school under the Monroe street
bridge"


One of these engineers, Mr.. Ken
nedy, was cross examined by the
councilmen with the effect that
new light was shed on the probable
cost of the work as outlined by Mr..
Ralston, and il was the admissions
of Mr., Kennedy that agitated the
councilmen. Kennedy said that to
install cable equipment will cost
$4,000, and that by this process
the concrete work can be reduced
to cost of 81 per cubic yard.
Without the galloway Kennedy
said the cost viii be si2 per yard,
whereas the councilmen said they
